relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus comes down from the mountain and the crowds follow.
A man asks not whether Jesus can heal, but whether he is willing.
Jesus touches what others avoid and cleansing happens.
The healing is sent back through testimony and priestly witness.
A Roman officer approaches in humility.
He carries concern for a suffering servant.
Jesus is willing to come.
The centurion trusts the power of a word under authority.
He understands command because he lives inside command.
Jesus marvels at trust found outside Israel’s expected borders.
Many from east and west will share the feast with the ancestors.
Inherited status alone cannot replace living trust.
As he trusted, so it becomes; the servant is healed.
Jesus enters Peter’s house and sees fever.
Touch restores her, and service flows from healing.
At evening, the suffering gather, and his word brings release and healing.
Isaiah’s vision of carried weakness becomes visible.
Jesus moves toward the other side.
A scribe offers total following.
The Son of Humanity walks without worldly security.
Another disciple asks for delay.
The call to follow presses with urgency.
The disciples enter the boat with him.
A storm covers the boat while Jesus sleeps.
Fear cries out for saving.
Jesus names little trust and speaks calm over wind and sea.
The disciples wonder at authority even over chaos-waters.
On the other side, tormented men emerge from tomb-space.
The unclean forces recognize the Son and fear the appointed time.
A herd feeds nearby.
The forces ask to be sent into the herd.
Jesus releases the men, and the disorder rushes into the sea.
The keepers flee and tell the city.
The city sees the power and asks Jesus to leave.
